question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cfg.network='other' branch in newClient() by controlling the mirrorRest URL to point to a server that responds slowly (just under mirrorNodeClientTimeout), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to consume the full timeout on each attempt and delay client initialization beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of HTTPS enforcement in the http.Client used by buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to perform a protocol downgrade attack, serving a plaintext HTTP response with adversarial node endpoints that route transactions to slow hosts, delaying block submission beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of context deadline propagation into the http.Client timeout in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() so that even after the parent context is cancelled, in-flight HTTP requests continue to block, causing the pinger to stall and freeze transaction submission for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror REST response that returns HTTP 503 (which is >= 500) on every attempt, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to retry with exponential backoff on each attempt, and with a sufficiently large mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries, accumulate enough delay to freeze transaction submission for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror REST response with a Content-Type that causes json.NewDecoder to misparse the body, returning a decode error that is treated as non-retryable (retry=false) in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork(), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to immediately fail and newClient() to abort, freezing all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) call in newClient() by providing a network map with only one entry pointing to a high-latency host, causing the SDK to route all transactions through that single node and delay block submission beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that newClient() does not validate the network map returned by buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() for minimum node count, so that a single adversarial node in the map becomes the sole transaction target, creating a single point of failure that can be taken offline to freeze block submission beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror REST response where all nodeEntry.ServiceEndpoints have DomainName values that are extremely long strings (e.g., 10,000 characters), causing net.JoinHostPort() and subsequent DNS resolution to consume excessive time, stalling fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() and delaying transaction submission beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror REST response with a nodesEnvelope containing duplicate network map entries (same address, different AccountID), causing the network map in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to be overwritten with the last entry, potentially replacing a valid node with an adversarial one and routing all transactions to a slow host that delays block submission beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the max(cfg.mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries + 1, 1) calculation in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() by causing the mirror REST server to always return retryable errors, so that with a large configured retry count the total wait time (sum of exponential backoffs) exceeds 500% of average block time before any transaction is submitted?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of response body size validation in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to serve a JSON response with a nodes array containing millions of entries, causing the decode and iteration loop to consume all available memory and trigger OOM, crashing the pinger and freezing all transaction submissions indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cfg.network switch in newClient() by injecting a network name that falls through to the default error case, causing newClient() to return an error and the pinger to call log.Fatalf(), terminating the process and freezing all transaction submissions permanently?
